A public USB charging port can be unsafe sometimes because a USB cable doesnt only carry electricity it can also transfer data If someone has secretly modified that charging port it might try to communicate with your phone when you plug it in In rare cases it could attempt to access information or send harmful software to the device This kind of attack is often called different names one is being USBBased Attack Use your own original charger brick or an authorized brand brick and cable to stay safe

Hey if youre thinking about moving for these taxfree jobs make sure you understand expat tax rules especially the Foreign Earned Income Exclusion FEIE It really helps people working abroad Check out requirements like the physical presence test to qualify Also look into the benefits package and cost of living in these places Living in the Middle East can be pretty different from the US so think about your lifestyle needs For daytoday costs check expat forums or local expat groups for real insights If youre serious it might be worth consulting a tax professional who knows international tax law Good luck
